## SECTION 4 — India National Updates
The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India aggressively continued to paralyze the national security apparatus. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 6, 1860, Abraham Lincoln, the incredibly massive, fiercely articulate, and highly anti-slavery candidate of the newly formed Republican Party, was officially and aggressively elected as the 16th President of the United States. His incredible, massive electoral victory instantly and fiercely triggered the absolute, catastrophic secession of southern slave-holding states, fundamentally and absolutely launching the incredibly brutal, massive carnage of the American Civil War that deeply threatened the entire survival of the American republic.

On November 6, 1917, the incredibly massive, immensely bloody, and historically definitive Battle of Passchendaele officially and aggressively concluded during World War I. Following months of absolute, horrific trench warfare in incredibly deep, massive mud, the Canadian Corps successfully and aggressively captured the devastated massive village. The incredibly massive strategic gain cost hundreds of thousands of absolute, horrific casualties on both sides, fiercely cementing Passchendaele as the absolute, enduring symbol of the senseless, massive slaughter on the Western Front.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 6, 1913, the incredibly massive, deeply horrific, and historically pivotal geopolitical confrontation known as the massive Gandhi-Smuts Agreement was fiercely sparked. Following the incredibly massive, brutal suppression of Indian mine workers on strike by the South African government led by Jan Smuts, Mahatma Gandhi aggressively initiated a massive, fiercely determined march. This absolute, uncompromising civil disobedience eventually forced Smuts into massive negotiations, fundamentally resulting in the Indian Relief Act of 1914, heavily securing massive concessions for the oppressed Indian diaspora.

On November 6, 1943, Subhas Chandra Bose, the incredibly massive, fiercely militant leader of the Indian National Army (INA), aggressively achieved an absolute, highly strategic milestone. At the Greater East Asia Conference in Tokyo, the massive Japanese Empire officially and aggressively handed over the absolute, administrative control of the massive Andaman and Nicobar Islands to the newly formed Azad Hind government. Bose triumphantly and heavily renamed them "Shaheed" (Martyr) and "Swaraj" (Independence) Islands, aggressively establishing the very first massive, sovereign Indian territory during World War II.
